response.justification: The candidate is a senior/staff-level engineer with 15+ years of experience applying for an entry-level junior role, making this a severe overqualification and retention risk. Java is listed only as a peripheral skill in a stack dominated by JS/TS and DLang, and German proficiency is self-rated as basic — well below the 'strong verbal and written' requirement for client-facing insurance consulting in Germany. Location in Lagos also conflicts with the in-Germany travel expectation.
strengths: Deep software craftsmanship background (TDD, CI/CD, agile)
Broad full-stack and architecture experience across many domains
Fluent English plus prior Berlin-based work history
concerns: Massively overqualified for an entry-level junior position
Only basic German vs. required strong German for insurance clients
Java is a minor skill; no insurance-sector experience and based in Lagos

key_factors.factor: Java not a primary skill, no insurance domain exposure
